description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical body, slightly bulging at the center and tapering towards the top and bottom. The barrel is constructed from vertical wooden staves, each tightly bound together by three dark metal hoops—one near the top, one at the center, and one near the bottom. The wood has a rich, medium-brown finish with visible grain, giving it a rustic and sturdy appearance. The top and bottom surfaces are flat and made from the same wood as the staves, with no visible openings or spouts. There are no handles or spigots, and the surface is unmarked by any labels or symbols. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage areas, or near taverns and markets.
description: A classic wooden barrel with dark metal hoops, bulging sides, and a flat wooden top and bottom. The wood is medium-brown and unmarked, giving it a rustic, sturdy look.
description_view_0: The barrel is seen from a slightly elevated angle, showing the flat wooden top and the curvature of the staves with three metal hoops.
description_view_1: This view highlights the side curvature and the even spacing of the metal hoops, with the wood grain clearly visible.
description_view_2: The barrel's bulging midsection and the symmetry of the metal bands are prominent from this angle.
description_view_3: Similar to other views, this angle emphasizes the smooth, unmarked wooden surface and the secure metal reinforcement.
